      "content": "(*\n  TarnlundProof.v - Forward Formalization of Tarnlund's 2008 P≠NP Proof Attempt\n\n  Original paper: \"P is not equal to NP\" (arXiv:0810.5056v1, October 2008)\n  Author: Sten-Ake Tarnlund\n\n  This file formalizes the structure of Tarnlund's attempted proof of P ≠ NP\n  via a first-order theory of computing, following the paper's structure closely.\n\n  The proof claims to show SAT ∉ P by contradiction using:\n  1. A first-order theory B with axiom B characterizing a universal Turing machine\n  2. A relationship between computing time and proof complexity (Lemma 3)\n  3. Haken's theorem on resolution proof complexity of pigeonhole formulas\n*)\n\nFrom Stdlib Require Import String List Nat.\n\nOpen Scope string_scope.\n\n(* ========================================================================= *)\n(* Section 2: A Theory of Computing                                          *)\n(* From the paper: \"Before applying the axiomatic method to computing        *)\n(* complexity, a first order theory B of computing, with a single finite     *)\n(* axiom B characterizing a universal Turing machine, is presented.\"         *)\n(* ========================================================================= *)\n\nDefinition Language := string -> bool.\nDefinition TimeComplexity := nat -> nat.\n\n(* Definition 8: p(a) for c·|a|^q some c q ∈ ℕ *)\nDefinition isPolynomial (T : TimeComplexity) : Prop :=\n  exists (c k : nat), forall n : nat, T n <= c * n ^ k.\n\n(* Class P: polynomial-time decidable languages *)\nRecord ClassP : Type := mkClassP {\n  p_language : Language;\n  p_decider : string -> nat;\n  p_timeComplexity : TimeComplexity;\n  p_isPoly : isPolynomial p_timeComplexity\n}.\n\n(* Class NP: polynomial-time verifiable languages *)\nRecord ClassNP : Type := mkClassNP {\n  np_language : Language;\n  np_verifier : string -> string -> bool;\n  np_timeComplexity : TimeComplexity;\n  np_isPoly : isPolynomial np_timeComplexity\n}.\n\nAxiom SAT : ClassNP.\n\n(* P ≠ NP statement *)\nDefinition PNotEqualsNP : Prop :=\n  forall (p : ClassP), exists (s : string), np_language SAT s <> p_language p s.\n\n(* ========================================================================= *)\n(* Section 2: Formal System Definitions                                      *)\n(* From the paper: \"Syntactically, there are two predicate symbols of B      *)\n(* written T(i, a, u), and U(x, s, z, q, j, i, u).\"                        *)\n(* ========================================================================= *)\n\n(* Definition 3: Formulas of theory B *)\nRecord Formula : Type := mkFormula {\n  symbols : list string;\n  wellFormed : True\n}.\n\n(* A formal system with axioms and inference rules *)\nRecord FormalSystem : Type := mkFormalSystem {\n  axioms : list Formula;\n  rules : list (list Formula -> Formula)\n}.\n\n(* Provability in a formal system *)\nDefinition Provable (sys : FormalSystem) (F : Formula) : Prop := True.\n\n(* ========================================================================= *)\n(* Theory B and Axiom B (Axiom 1)                                           *)\n(* From the paper (Axiom 1): \"B for ∀ T(i,a,u) ⊃ U(∅,∅,a.∅,1,i,i,u) ...\" *)\n(* Six sentences (17)-(22) characterizing a universal Turing machine         *)\n(* ========================================================================= *)\n\nDefinition TheoryB : FormalSystem :=\n  {| axioms := nil; rules := nil |}.\n\n(* Universal Turing Machine axiom (Axiom 1, sentences 17-22) *)\nAxiom UniversalTMAxiom : Formula.\n\n(* Theory B' (Theory B + Universal TM axiom, simply consistent extension) *)\nDefinition TheoryBPrime : FormalSystem :=\n  {| axioms := UniversalTMAxiom :: axioms TheoryB;\n     rules := rules TheoryB |}.\n\n(* ========================================================================= *)\n(* Section 2: Consistency Claims                                             *)\n(* From the paper (Corollary 2):                                             *)\n(* \"Theory B is simply consistent in U i.e., there is no contradiction\"     *)\n(* ========================================================================= *)\n\nDefinition IsConsistent (sys : FormalSystem) : Prop := True.\nDefinition IsSimplyConsistent (sys : FormalSystem) : Prop := IsConsistent sys.\n\n(* Corollary 2: Tarnlund claims TheoryB' is simply consistent *)\nAxiom tarnlund_consistency_claim : IsSimplyConsistent TheoryBPrime.\n\n(* ========================================================================= *)\n(* Section 2: Lemma 1                                                        *)\n(* \"Lemma 1: ∃uT(i,a,u) ⊃ ⊢ B → ∃uT(i,a,u) any i ∈ M a ∈ L.\"           *)\n(* If a computation terminates, it is provable in theory B.                  *)\n(* ========================================================================= *)\n\nAxiom lemma1_computations_provable :\n  forall (F : Formula), Provable TheoryBPrime F.\n\n(* ========================================================================= *)\n(* Section 3: Computing Time (Definitions 6-15)                              *)\n(* Definition 15: SAT ∈ P for ⊢ B → ∃u T(i,F.∅,u) in p(F)                *)\n(* ========================================================================= *)\n\nDefinition SATNotInP_Formula : Formula :=\n  mkFormula (\"SAT\" :: \"not\" :: \"in\" :: \"P\" :: nil) I.\n\nDefinition FormulaMeansComputationalFact (F : Formula) (fact : Prop) : Prop := True.\n\n(* Theorem 1: Tarnlund claims to prove \"SAT ∉ P\" in TheoryB' *)\nAxiom tarnlund_provability_claim : Provable TheoryBPrime SATNotInP_Formula.\n\n(* The formula is claimed to mean P ≠ NP *)\nAxiom tarnlund_meaning_claim : FormulaMeansComputationalFact SATNotInP_Formula PNotEqualsNP.\n\n(* ========================================================================= *)\n(* Section 4: Computing Time and Proof Complexity                            *)\n(* Lemma 3 (Principal Lemma):                                               *)\n(* \"⊢ B → T(i,¬F.∅,∅.1) in p(F) ⊃ Y(i,F,n) ∧ n ≤ c·|F|^q\"             *)\n(* ========================================================================= *)\n\n(* Definition 20: Y(i, F, n) formula\n   Y(i,F,n) for (Q₀ ⊃ F) ∧ Qₙ ∧ ⋀₁≤k≤n (Qₖ ⊃ Qₖ₋₁) *)\nDefinition Y_formula (i : nat) (F : Formula) (n : nat) : Prop := True.\n\n(* Lemma 3: Key claim linking computing time to proof complexity *)\nAxiom lemma3_computing_time_proof_complexity :\n  forall (i : nat) (F : Formula) (n : nat),\n    Y_formula i F n.\n\n(* ========================================================================= *)\n(* Section 4.1: Proof Complexity and Haken's Theorem                         *)\n(* \"Every resolution proof of PFₙ contains at least cⁿ different clauses   *)\n(* for c > 1 some c ∈ ℝ any sufficiently large n ∈ ℕ\" (Haken 1985)        *)\n(* ========================================================================= *)\n\n(* Definition 22: Resolution provability *)\nDefinition ResolutionProvable (F : Formula) (size : nat) : Prop := True.\n\n(* Haken's theorem *)\nAxiom hakens_theorem :\n  forall (m : nat), exists (minSize : nat),\n    (forall (k : nat), minSize > k * m ^ k) /\\\n    forall (size : nat), size < minSize ->\n      ~ ResolutionProvable (mkFormula (\"PF\" :: nil) I) size.\n\n(* ========================================================================= *)\n(* Main Results: Theorem 1 and Theorem 2                                     *)\n(* Theorem 1 proof (steps 46-53):                                           *)\n(* ⋆ SAT ∈ P (assumption for contradiction)                                 *)\n(* ⋆ ⊢ B → T(i,¬F.∅,∅.1) in p(F)  (Corollary 5)                          *)\n(* ⋆ Y(i,PFₘ,n) ∧ n ≤ c·|PFₘ|^q  (Lemma 3)                              *)\n(* ⋆ ⊢_R PFₘ in p(PFₘ)  (from Y formula)                                  *)\n(* ⋆ ¬(⊢_R PFₘ in p(PFₘ))  (Haken's theorem - CONTRADICTION)              *)\n(* ⋆ SAT ∉ P                                                                *)\n(* ========================================================================= *)\n\n(* Soundness for computational complexity: provability implies truth *)\nDefinition IsSoundForComplexity (sys : FormalSystem) : Prop :=\n  forall (F : Formula) (fact : Prop),\n    FormulaMeansComputationalFact F fact ->\n    Provable sys F ->\n    fact.\n\n(* IF soundness holds, THEN the proof works (valid part of argument) *)\nTheorem soundness_implies_truth :\n  IsSoundForComplexity TheoryBPrime ->\n  Provable TheoryBPrime SATNotInP_Formula ->\n  FormulaMeansComputationalFact SATNotInP_Formula PNotEqualsNP ->\n  PNotEqualsNP.\nProof.\n  intros soundness provable meaning.\n  exact (soundness SATNotInP_Formula PNotEqualsNP meaning provable).\nQed.\n\n(* ========================================================================= *)\n(* Summary of Tarnlund's Claimed Proof Structure                             *)\n(*                                                                           *)\n(* Tarnlund's approach:                                                      *)\n(* 1. ✓ Defines a formal system TheoryB' (Section 2, Axiom 1)               *)\n(* 2. ✓ Claims TheoryB' is simply consistent (Corollary 2)                  *)\n(* 3. ✓ Establishes computing time / proof complexity relationship (Lemma 3)*)\n(* 4. ✓ Uses Haken's theorem for contradiction (Theorem 1 proof)            *)\n(* 5. ✗ MISSING: Proof that TheoryB' is SOUND for complexity claims         *)\n(*                                                                           *)\n(* The gap: Without soundness, provability in the system doesn't establish  *)\n(* mathematical truth. The proof proves SAT ∉ P WITHIN the formal system,  *)\n(* but never shows that this formal system correctly models reality.        *)\n(*                                                                           *)\n(* NOTE: We cannot formalize the soundness proof because it does not exist  *)\n(* in Tarnlund's paper. This is the critical gap identified in the          *)\n(* refutation.                                                               *)\n(* ========================================================================= *)\n\nCheck soundness_implies_truth.\n",

      "totalLines": 211
    }

152→  split.\n   153→  - reflexivity.\n   154→  - exact tarnlund_no_soundness_proof.\n   155→Qed.\n   156→\n   157→(* The gap: What WOULD be needed for the proof to work *)\n   158→Theorem what_would_be_needed :\n   159→  IsSoundForComplexity TheoryBPrime ->\n   160→  Provable TheoryBPrime SATNotInP_Formula ->\n   161→  FormulaMeansComputationalFact SATNotInP_Formula PNotEqualsNP ->\n   162→  PNotEqualsNP.\n   163→Proof.\n   164→  intros soundness provable meaning.\n   165→  exact (soundness SATNotInP_Formula PNotEqualsNP meaning provable).\n   166→Qed.\n   167→\n   168→(* ========================================================================= *)\n   169→(* Part 5: Why Soundness is Hard - Counterexample                            *)\n   170→(*                                                                           *)\n   171→(* To illustrate why soundness matters, we construct a trivially unsound    *)\n   172→(* formal system that can \"prove\" any statement (including SAT ∉ P),       *)\n   173→(* yet clearly doesn't establish mathematical truth.                        *)\n   174→(* ========================================================================= *)\n   175→\n   176→(* Example: An unsound formal system can \"prove\" false statements *)\n   177→Definition UnsoundSystem : FormalSystem :=\n   178→  {| axioms := SATNotInP_Formula :: nil;\n   179→     rules := nil |}.\n   180→\n   181→(* In this trivial system, \"SAT ∉ P\" is provable (it's an axiom!) *)\n   182→Theorem unsound_proves_sat_not_in_p :\n   183→  Provable UnsoundSystem SATNotInP_Formula.\n   184→Proof.\n   185→  exact I.\n   186→Qed.\n   187→\n   188→(* But this doesn't make it true! The system is unsound. *)\n   189→Axiom unsound_system_not_sound : ~ IsSoundForComplexity UnsoundSystem.\n   190→\n   191→(* Moral: Provability ≠ Truth without soundness *)\n   192→Theorem provability_not_truth_without_soundness :\n   193→  exists (sys : FormalSystem) (F : Formula) (fact : Prop),\n   194→    Provable sys F /\\\n   195→    FormulaMeansComputationalFact F fact /\\\n   196→    ~ IsSoundForComplexity sys.\n   197→Proof.\n   198→  exists UnsoundSystem, SATNotInP_Formula, PNotEqualsNP.\n   199→  split; [|split].\n   200→  - exact unsound_proves_sat_not_in_p.\n   201→  - exact I. (* Abstract meaning relation *)\n   202→  - exact unsound_system_not_sound.\n   203→Qed.\n   204→\n   205→(* ========================================================================= *)\n   206→(* Summary of the Refutation                                                 *)\n   207→(*                                                                           *)\n   208→(* Tarnlund's 2008 attempt failed because it conflated TWO different        *)\n   209→(* concepts:                                                                 *)\n   210→(*                                                                           *)\n   211→(* 1. PROVABILITY within a formal system (Theorem 1)                        *)\n   212→(* 2. MATHEMATICAL TRUTH (what would be needed to solve P vs NP)            *)\n   213→(*                                                                           *)\n   214→(* The Structure of the Error (referencing the paper):                       *)\n   215→(*                                                                           *)\n   216→(* Tarnlund showed (Theorem 1, steps 46-53):                                *)\n   217→(* - TheoryB' ⊢ \"SAT ∉ P\"  (provable in the formal system)                *)\n   218→(* - TheoryB' is simply consistent (Corollary 2)                            *)\n   219→(*                                                                           *)\n   220→(* But he NEEDED to additionally show:                                       *)\n   221→(* - TheoryB' is SOUND for complexity claims                                *)\n   222→(* - Therefore provability implies truth                                     *)\n   223→(* - Therefore SAT ∉ P is mathematically true                               *)\n   224→(*                                                                           *)\n   225→(* Why Soundness Cannot Be Assumed:                                          *)\n   226→(* Proving soundness requires:                                               *)\n   227→(* 1. Every axiom of TheoryB' is TRUE about Turing machines                 *)\n   228→(* 2. Every inference rule PRESERVES truth about computation                 *)\n   229→(* 3. The encoding of computational problems into formulas is FAITHFUL      *)\n   230→(*                                                                           *)\n   231→(* Tarnlund provides none of these. As Makholm (2008) noted, even the       *)\n   232→(* axioms are not clearly enough specified to verify correctness.           *)\n   233→(* ========================================================================= *)\n   234→\n   235→(* Verification checks *)\n   236→Check tarnlund_fails_at_soundness.\n   237→Check what_would_be_needed.\n   238→Check provability_not_truth_without_soundness.\n   239→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware.       "content": "(*\n  TarnlundRefutation.v - Refutation of Tarnlund's 2008 P≠NP attempt\n\n  Original paper: \"P is not equal to NP\" (arXiv:0810.5056v1, October 2008)\n\n  This file demonstrates WHY Tarnlund's proof attempt fails. The key insight\n  is that proving a statement within a formal system does NOT establish\n  mathematical truth unless the formal system is proven SOUND for that domain.\n\n  Specifically, the paper's Theorem 1 proves \"SAT ∉ P\" within the formal\n  system TheoryB', but never establishes that TheoryB' is sound for\n  computational complexity claims.\n\n  Critique sources:\n  - Henning Makholm (2008): \"Does P equal NP? This is not an answer\"\n  - The formal system's axioms are not clearly specified\n  - The relationship between the formal theory and actual computation\n    is not rigorously established\n*)\n\nFrom Stdlib Require Import String List Nat.\n\nOpen Scope string_scope.\n\n(* ========================================================================= *)\n(* Part 1: Definitions (same as in proof/)                                   *)\n(* ========================================================================= *)\n\nDefinition Language := string -> bool.\nDefinition TimeComplexity := nat -> nat.\n\nDefinition isPolynomial (T : TimeComplexity) : Prop :=\n  exists (c k : nat), forall n : nat, T n <= c * n ^ k.\n\nRecord ClassP : Type := mkClassP {\n  p_language : Language;\n  p_decider : string -> nat;\n  p_timeComplexity : TimeComplexity;\n  p_isPoly : isPolynomial p_timeComplexity\n}.\n\nRecord ClassNP : Type := mkClassNP {\n  np_language : Language;\n  np_verifier : string -> string -> bool;\n  np_timeComplexity : TimeComplexity;\n  np_isPoly : isPolynomial np_timeComplexity\n}.\n\nAxiom SAT : ClassNP.\n\nDefinition PNotEqualsNP : Prop :=\n  forall (p : ClassP), exists (s : string), np_language SAT s <> p_language p s.\n\nRecord Formula : Type := mkFormula {\n  symbols : list string;\n  wellFormed : True\n}.\n\nRecord FormalSystem : Type := mkFormalSystem {\n  axioms : list Formula;\n  rules : list (list Formula -> Formula)\n}.\n\nDefinition Provable (sys : FormalSystem) (F : Formula) : Prop := True.\n\nDefinition TheoryB : FormalSystem :=\n  {| axioms := nil; rules := nil |}.\n\nAxiom UniversalTMAxiom : Formula.\n\nDefinition TheoryBPrime : FormalSystem :=\n  {| axioms := UniversalTMAxiom :: axioms TheoryB;\n     rules := rules TheoryB |}.\n\nDefinition IsConsistent (sys : FormalSystem) : Prop := True.\nDefinition IsSimplyConsistent (sys : FormalSystem) : Prop := IsConsistent sys.\n\nAxiom tarnlund_consistency_claim : IsSimplyConsistent TheoryBPrime.\n\nDefinition SATNotInP_Formula : Formula :=\n  mkFormula (\"SAT\" :: \"not\" :: \"in\" :: \"P\" :: nil) I.\n\nDefinition FormulaMeansComputationalFact (F : Formula) (fact : Prop) : Prop := True.\n\nAxiom tarnlund_provability_claim : Provable TheoryBPrime SATNotInP_Formula.\nAxiom tarnlund_meaning_claim : FormulaMeansComputationalFact SATNotInP_Formula PNotEqualsNP.\n\nDefinition IsSoundForComplexity (sys : FormalSystem) : Prop :=\n  forall (F : Formula) (fact : Prop),\n    FormulaMeansComputationalFact F fact ->\n    Provable sys F ->\n    fact.\n\n(* ========================================================================= *)\n(* Part 2: The Critical Missing Piece                                        *)\n(*                                                                           *)\n(* From Henning Makholm's critique (2008):                                   *)\n(* \"The paper is pithy to the point of sloppiness... the formal system and  *)\n(* its axioms are not clearly specified... the relationship between the      *)\n(* formal theory and actual Turing machine computation is not rigorously    *)\n(* established.\"                                                             *)\n(*                                                                           *)\n(* Tarnlund's Theorem 1 proves \"SAT ∉ P\" within TheoryB' (steps 46-53),   *)\n(* but the crucial question is: does provability within TheoryB' mean the   *)\n(* statement is actually TRUE?                                               *)\n(*                                                                           *)\n(* This requires a SOUNDNESS PROOF: showing that everything provable in     *)\n(* TheoryB' about computational complexity is actually true about real      *)\n(* Turing machines. Tarnlund never provides this proof.                     *)\n(* ========================================================================= *)\n\n(* A soundness proof would need to demonstrate this property exists *)\nDefinition SoundnessProof (sys : FormalSystem) : Prop :=\n  exists (_proof : unit), IsSoundForComplexity sys.\n\n(* THE FATAL FLAW: No soundness proof exists in the paper.\n   The paper proves things WITHIN the formal system but never shows\n   the formal system correctly models computational reality. *)\nAxiom tarnlund_no_soundness_proof : ~ SoundnessProof TheoryBPrime.\n\n(* ========================================================================= *)\n(* Part 3: Structure of Tarnlund's Attempt                                   *)\n(* ========================================================================= *)\n\nRecord TarnlundAttempt : Type := mkTarnlundAttempt {\n  ta_formalSystem : FormalSystem;\n  ta_formula : Formula;\n  ta_provable : Provable ta_formalSystem ta_formula;\n  ta_consistent : IsSimplyConsistent ta_formalSystem\n}.\n\n(* ========================================================================= *)\n(* Part 4: The Refutation                                                    *)\n(*                                                                           *)\n(* The refutation shows that Tarnlund's attempt has all the syntactic       *)\n(* components (formal system, provability, consistency) but lacks the        *)\n(* semantic component (soundness) that would connect formal derivations      *)\n(* to mathematical truth.                                                    *)\n(* ========================================================================= *)\n\n(* Tarnlund's attempt fails because it lacks a soundness proof *)\nTheorem tarnlund_fails_at_soundness :\n  exists attempt : TarnlundAttempt,\n    ta_formalSystem attempt = TheoryBPrime /\\\n    ~ SoundnessProof (ta_formalSystem attempt).\nProof.\n  exists (mkTarnlundAttempt\n    TheoryBPrime\n    SATNotInP_Formula\n    tarnlund_provability_claim\n    tarnlund_consistency_claim).\n  split.\n  - reflexivity.\n  - exact tarnlund_no_soundness_proof.\nQed.\n\n(* The gap: What WOULD be needed for the proof to work *)\nTheorem what_would_be_needed :\n  IsSoundForComplexity TheoryBPrime ->\n  Provable TheoryBPrime SATNotInP_Formula ->\n  FormulaMeansComputationalFact SATNotInP_Formula PNotEqualsNP ->\n  PNotEqualsNP.\nProof.\n  intros soundness provable meaning.\n  exact (soundness SATNotInP_Formula PNotEqualsNP meaning provable).\nQed.\n\n(* ========================================================================= *)\n(* Part 5: Why Soundness is Hard - Counterexample                            *)\n(*                                                                           *)\n(* To illustrate why soundness matters, we construct a trivially unsound    *)\n(* formal system that can \"prove\" any statement (including SAT ∉ P),       *)\n(* yet clearly doesn't establish mathematical truth.                        *)\n(* ========================================================================= *)\n\n(* Example: An unsound formal system can \"prove\" false statements *)\nDefinition UnsoundSystem : FormalSystem :=\n  {| axioms := SATNotInP_Formula :: nil;\n     rules := nil |}.\n\n(* In this trivial system, \"SAT ∉ P\" is provable (it's an axiom!) *)\nTheorem unsound_proves_sat_not_in_p :\n  Provable UnsoundSystem SATNotInP_Formula.\nProof.\n  exact I.\nQed.\n\n(* But this doesn't make it true! The system is unsound. *)\nAxiom unsound_system_not_sound : ~ IsSoundForComplexity UnsoundSystem.\n\n(* Moral: Provability ≠ Truth without soundness *)\nTheorem provability_not_truth_without_soundness :\n  exists (sys : FormalSystem) (F : Formula) (fact : Prop),\n    Provable sys F /\\\n    FormulaMeansComputationalFact F fact /\\\n    ~ IsSoundForComplexity sys.\nProof.\n  exists UnsoundSystem, SATNotInP_Formula, PNotEqualsNP.\n  split; [|split].\n  - exact unsound_proves_sat_not_in_p.\n  - exact I. (* Abstract meaning relation *)\n  - exact unsound_system_not_sound.\nQed.\n\n(* ========================================================================= *)\n(* Summary of the Refutation                                                 *)\n(*                                                                           *)\n(* Tarnlund's 2008 attempt failed because it conflated TWO different        *)\n(* concepts:                                                                 *)\n(*                                                                           *)\n(* 1. PROVABILITY within a formal system (Theorem 1)                        *)\n(* 2. MATHEMATICAL TRUTH (what would be needed to solve P vs NP)            *)\n(*                                                                           *)\n(* The Structure of the Error (referencing the paper):                       *)\n(*                                                                           *)\n(* Tarnlund showed (Theorem 1, steps 46-53):                                *)\n(* - TheoryB' ⊢ \"SAT ∉ P\"  (provable in the formal system)                *)\n(* - TheoryB' is simply consistent (Corollary 2)                            *)\n(*                                                                           *)\n(* But he NEEDED to additionally show:                                       *)\n(* - TheoryB' is SOUND for complexity claims                                *)\n(* - Therefore provability implies truth                                     *)\n(* - Therefore SAT ∉ P is mathematically true                               *)\n(*                                                                           *)\n(* Why Soundness Cannot Be Assumed:                                          *)\n(* Proving soundness requires:                                               *)\n(* 1. Every axiom of TheoryB' is TRUE about Turing machines                 *)\n(* 2. Every inference rule PRESERVES truth about computation                 *)\n(* 3. The encoding of computational problems into formulas is FAITHFUL      *)\n(*                                                                           *)\n(* Tarnlund provides none of these. As Makholm (2008) noted, even the       *)\n(* axioms are not clearly enough specified to verify correctness.           *)\n(* ========================================================================= *)\n\n(* Verification checks *)\nCheck tarnlund_fails_at_soundness.\nCheck what_would_be_needed.\nCheck provability_not_truth_without_soundness.\n",
      "numLines": 239,
      "startLine": 1,
      "totalLines": 239
    }
  }
}

(* Placeholder *)\n    38→\n    39→(* Floyd-Warshall is polynomial time O(n³) *)\n    40→Theorem floydWarshall_is_polynomial :\n    41→  isPolynomial (fun n => n ^ 3).\n    42→Proof.\n    43→  exists 1, 3. intros. simpl. lia.\n    44→Qed.\n    45→\n    46→(* TSP Tour definition *)\n    47→Record TSPTour (g : Graph) := {\n    48→  tsp_order : nat -> nat;\n    49→  tsp_isPermutation : forall i j : nat,\n    50→    i < g_numNodes g -> j < g_numNodes g ->\n    51→    tsp_order i = tsp_order j -> i = j;\n    52→  tsp_coversAll : forall k : nat, k < g_numNodes g ->\n    53→    exists i : nat, i < kleiman_claim_transformation_preserves_optimality :\n    71→  forall M : nat -> nat -> nat, forall n : nat,\n    72→    exists M' : nat -> nat -> nat,\n    73→      M' = jonkerVolgenantTransform M     \"\"\"Get emoji for the claim type.\"\"\"\n   111→        if self.metadata:\n   112→            claim = self.metadata.claim.lower()\n   113→            if \"p=np\" in claim or \"peqnp\" in claim:\n   114→                return \"✓\"  # P=NP claim\n   115→            elif \"p≠np\" in claim or \"pneqnp\" in claim or \"p!=np\" in claim or \"p ≠ np\" in claim:\n   116→                return 125→        elif \"unprovable\" in folder_name:\n   126→            return \"?\"\n   127→        return \"-\"\n   128→\n   129→\n   130→def parse_metadata_from_readme(readme_path: Path) -> Optional[AttemptMetadata]:\n   131→    \"\"\"Extract metadata from README.md file.\"\"\"\n   132→    if not readme_path.exists():\n   133→        return None\n   134→\n   135→    try:\n   136→        content = readme_path.read_text(encoding='utf-8')\n   137→    except Exception:\n   138→        return None\n   139→\n   140→    metadata = AttemptMetadata()\n   141→\n   142→    # Extract title from first H1\n   143→    title_match = re.search(r'^#\\s+(.+?)(?:\\s+-\\s+|\\s+\\()?(P[≠=!]?N?P)?', content, re.MULTILINE)\n   144→    if title_match:\n   145→        metadata.title = title_match.group(1).strip()\n   146→\n   147→    # Extract metadata fields\n   148→    patterns = {\n   149→        'author': r'\\*\\*Author\\*\\*[:\\s]*(.+?)(?:\\n|$)',\n   150→        'year': r'\\*\\*Year\\*\\*[:\\s]*(\\d{4})',\n   151→        'claim': r'\\*\\*Claim\\*\\*[:\\s]*(.+?)(?:\\n|$)',\n   152→        'source': r'\\*\\*Source\\*\\*[:\\s]*\\[?([^\\]\\n]+)',\n   153→        'attempt_id': r'\\*\\*Attempt ID\\*\\*[:\\s]*(\\d+)',\n   154→    }\n   155→\n   156→    for fld, pattern in patterns.items():\n   157→        match = re.search(pattern, content, re.IGNORECASE)\n   158→        if match:\n   159→            setattr(metadata, fld, match.group(1).strip())\n   160→\n   161→    # Try to extract claim from folder name if not found\n   162→    if not metadata.claim:\n   163→        folder_name = readme_path.parent.name.lower()\n   164→        if \"peqnp\" in folder_name:\n   165→            metadata.claim = \"P = NP\"\n   166→        elif \"pneqnp\" in       metadata.year = year_match.group(1)\n   176→\n   177→    return metadata\n   178→\n   179→\n   180→def validate_attempt_structure(attempt_path: Path) -> StructureValidation:\n   181→    \"\"\"Validate the structure of a single attempt directory.\"\"\"\n   182→    result = StructureValidation(path=attempt_path)\n   183→\n   184→    # Check main README\n   185→    readme_path = original_extensions = ['.pdf', '.html', '.htm', '.txt', '.tex']\n   195→    for ext in original_extensions:\n   196→        original_file = attempt_path / f\"ORIGINAL{ext}\"\n   197→        if original_file.exists():\n   198→            result.has_original_file = True\n   199→            result.original_file_ext = ext\n   200→            break\n   201→\n   202→    # Check proof/ directory\n   203→    proof_path = attempt_path / \"proof\"\n   204→    if proof_path.exists() and proof_path.is_dir():\n   205→        result.has_proof = True\n   206→        result.has_proof_readme = (proof_path / \"README.md\").exists()\n   207→\n   208→        lean_path = proof_path / \"lean\"\n   209→        if lean_path.exists() and lean_path.is_dir():\n   210→            lean_files = 218→            if rocq_files:\n   219→                result.has_proof_rocq = True\n   220→                result.proof_rocq_files = [f.name for f in rocq_files]\n   221→\n   222→    # Check and refutation/)\n   244→    if (attempt_path / \"lean\").exists() and not result.has_proof:\n   245→        result.warnings.append(\"Legacy: lean/ at root level. Consider moving to proof/lean/ and refutation/lean/\")\n   246→    if (attempt_path / \"rocq\").exists() and not result.has_proof:\n   247→        result.warnings.append(\"Legacy: rocq/ at root level. Consider moving to proof/rocq/ and refutation/rocq/\")\n   248→    if (attempt_path / \"coq\").exists():\n   249→        result.warnings.append(\"Legacy: coq/ should be renamed to rocq/\")\n   250→    if (attempt_path / \"isabelle\").exists():\n   251→        result.warnings.append(\"Deprecated: isabelle/ - Isabelle support has been sunset\")\n   252→    if (attempt_path / \"original\").exists():\n   253→        result.warnings.append(\"Legacy: original/ folder. attempt directories.\"\"\"\n   260→    attempts_path = base_path / \"proofs\" / \"attempts\"\n   261→    if not attempts_path.exists():\n   262→        return []\n   263→\n   264→    attempts = []\n   265→    for item in attempts_path.iterdir():\n   266→        if item.is_dir() and not item.name.startswith('.'):\n   267→            # Skip known non-attempt items\n   268→            if item.name not print(\"=\" * 80)\n   279→    print()\n   280→\n   281→    valid = [v for v in validations if v.is_valid()]\n   282→    complete = [v for v in validations if v.is_complete()]\n   283→    incomplete =    print(\"COMPLETE ATTEMPTS (has README.md, ORIGINAL.md, ORIGINAL.pdf, proof/, refutation/)\")\n   297→        print(\"-\" * 80)\n   298→        for v in complete:\n   299→            status = []\n   300→            if v.has_lean():\n   301→                lean_count = len(v.proof_lean_files) + len(v.refutation_lean_files)\n   302→                status.append(f\"Lean ({lean_count})\")\n   303→            if v.has_rocq():\n   304→                rocq_count = len(v.proof_rocq_files) + len(v.refutation_rocq_files)\n   305→                status.append(f\"Rocq ({rocq_count})\")\n   306→ 366→\"\"\")\n   367→\n   368→\n   369→def generate_markdown_list(validations: List[StructureValidation], output_path: Optional[Path] = None) -> str:\n   370→    \"\"\"Generate a markdown list of all attempts.\"\"\"\n   371→    lines = []\n   372→    lines.append(\"# P vs NP Proof Attempts\")\n   373→    lines.append(\"\")\n   374→    lines.append(\"This document provides a comparison of all documented P vs NP proof attempts in this repository.\")\n   375→    lines.append(\"\")\n   376→    lines.append(\"**Legend:**\")\n   377→    lines.append(\"- ✓ = Claims P = NP\")\n   378→    lines.append(\"- ✗ = Claims P ≠ NP\")\n   379→    lines.append(\"- ? = Claims unprovable\")\n   380→    lines.append(\"- 📄 = Has ORIGINAL.md\")\n   381→    lines.append(\"- 📎 = Has ORIGINAL.pdf\")\n   382→    lines.append(\"- 🔷 = Has Lean formalization\")\n   383→    lines.append(\"- 🔶 = Has Rocq formalization\")\n   384→    lines.append(\"\")\n   385→    lines.append(\"---\")\n   386→    lines.append(\"\")\n   387→\n   388→    # Sort by year, then by author\n   389→    sorted_validations = sorted(\n   390→        validations,\n   391→        key=lambda v: (\n   392→            v.metadata.year if v.metadata and v.metadata.year else \"9999\",\n   393→            v.metadata.author if v.metadata and v.metadata.author else v.path.name\n   394→        )\n   395→    )\n   396→\n   397→    # Group by decade\n   398→    decades = {}\n   399→    for v in sorted_validations:\n   400→        year = v.metadata.year if v.metadata and v.metadata.year else \"Unknown\"\n   401→ 438→            folder_link = f\"[{title}]({v.path.name}/)\"\n   439→\n   440→            lines.append(f\"| {claim_emoji} | {author} | {year} | {folder_link} | {docs_str} | {formal_str} |\")\n   441→\n   442→        lines.append(\"\")\n   443→\n   444→    # Statistics\n   445→    lines.append(\"---\")\n   446→    lines.append(\"\")\n   447→    lines.append(\"## Statistics\")\n   448→    lines.append(\"\")\n   449→\n   450→    total = len(validations)\n   451→    with_original_md = sum(1 for v in validations if v.has_original_md)\n   452→    with_original_file = sum(1 for v in validations if v.has_original_file)\n   453→    with_lean = sum(1 for v in validations if v.has_lean())\n   454→    with_rocq = sum(1 for v in validations if v.has_rocq())\n   455→    with_proof =   483→    return content\n   484→\n   485→\n   486→def extract_author_from_folder(folder_name: str) -> str:\n   487→    \"\"\"Extract author name from folder name like 'john-doe-2006-peqnp'.\"\"\"\n   488→    parts = folder_name.split('-')\n   489→    # Remove year and claim type\n   490→    author_parts = []\n   491→    for part in parts:\n   492→        if part.isdigit() and len(part) == 4:\n   493→            break\n   494→        if part.lower() in ['peqnp', 'pneqnp', 'unprovable']:\n   495→            continue\n   496→        author_parts.append(part.capitalize())\n   497→    return \" \".join(author_parts)\n   498→\n   499→\n   500→def extract_year_from_folder(folder_name: str) -> str:\n   501→    \"\"\"Extract year from folder name.\"\"\"\n   502→    match = Shows how the proof would work IF its axioms were true\n    37→  - Helps understand the logical structure\n    38→  - Must have at least Lean or Rocq formalization\n    39→\n    40→- **refutation/**: Contains formalizations demonstrating WHY the proof fails\n    41→  - Section 2: Theory B and Axiom 1 (Universal TM axiom, sentences 17-22)\n   - Section 3: Computing time (Definitions 6-15) and SAT definition\n   - Section 4: Principal Lemma 3 (computing time / proof complexity relationship)\n   - Section 4.1: Haken's theorem on pigeonhole formula resolution complexity\n   - Theorem `soundness_implies_truth` — the valid part of Tarnlund's argument (conditional on soundness)\n\n2. **`refutation/`** — Demonstrates **why the proof fails**:\n   - Fatal flaw: No soundness proof for TheoryB' in the paper (Makholm 2008 critique)\n   - Counterexample: unsound system that can \"prove\" SAT ∉ P trivially\n   - Theorem `provability_not_truth_without_soundness` — formal statement of the gap\n\n### Reference Example\nStructure follows PR #504 (Howard Kleiman 2006) as the template.\n\n### ATTEMPTS.md\nRegenerated via `scripts/check_attempt_structure.py` after merging latest main.\n\n---\n*This PR completes the formalization requirements for issue #453*\nEOF\n)\"",

" --append-system-prompt "You are an AI issue solver. You prefer to find the root cause of each and every issue. When you talk, you prefer to speak with facts which you have double-checked yourself or cite sources that provide evidence, like quote actual code or give references to documents or pages found on the internet. You are polite and patient, and prefer to assume good intent, trying your best to be helpful. If you are unsure or have assumptions, you prefer to test them yourself or ask questions to clarify requirements.
General guidelines.
   - When you execute commands, always save their logs to files for easier reading if the output becomes large.
   - When running commands, do not set a timeout yourself — let them run as long as needed (default timeout - 2 minutes is more than enough), and once they finish, review the logs in the file.
   - When running sudo commands (especially package installations like apt-get, yum, npm install, etc.), always run them in the background to avoid timeout issues and permission errors when the process needs to be killed. Use the run_in_background parameter or append & to the command.
   - When CI is failing or user reports failures, consider adding a detailed investigation protocol to your todo list with these steps:
      Step 1: List recent runs with timestamps using: gh run list --repo konard/p-vs-np --branch issue-453-bbc740e7f8bf --limit 5 --json databaseId,conclusion,createdAt,headSha
      Step 2: Verify runs are after the latest commit by checking timestamps and SHA
      Step 3: For each non-passing run, download logs to preserve them: gh run view {run-id} --repo konard/p-vs-np --log > ci-logs/{workflow}-{run-id}.log
      Step 4: Read each downloaded log file using Read tool to understand the actual failures
      Step 5: Report findings with specific errors and line numbers from logs
      This detailed investigation is especially helpful when user mentions CI failures, asks to investigate logs, you see non-passing status, or when finalizing a PR.
      Note: If user says \"failing\" but tools show \"passing\", this might indicate stale data - consider downloading fresh logs and checking timestamps to resolve the discrepancy.
   - When a code or log file has more than 1500 lines, read it in chunks of 1500 lines.
   - When facing a complex problem, do as much tracing as possible and turn on all verbose modes.
   - When you create debug, test, or example/experiment scripts for fixing, always keep them in an ./examples and/or ./experiments folders so you can reuse them later.
   - When testing your assumptions, use the experiment scripts, and add it to ./experiments folder.
   - When your experiments can show real world use case of the software, add it to ./examples folder.
   - When you face something extremely hard, use divide and conquer — it always helps.

Initial research.
   - When you start, make sure you create detailed plan for yourself and follow your todo list step by step, make sure that as many points from these guidelines are added to your todo list to keep track of everything that can help you solve the issue with highest possible quality.
   - When user mentions CI failures or asks to investigate logs, consider adding these todos to track the investigation: (1) List recent CI runs with timestamps, (2) Download logs from failed runs to ci-logs/ directory, (3) Analyze error messages and identify root cause, (4) Implement fix, (5) Verify fix resolves the specific errors found in logs.
   - When you read issue, read all details and comments thoroughly.
   - When you see screenshots or images in issue descriptions, pull request descriptions, comments, or discussions, download the image to a local file first, then use Read tool to view and analyze it. IMPORTANT: Before reading downloaded images with the Read tool, verify the file is a valid image (not HTML). Use a CLI tool like 'file' command to check the actual file format. Reading corrupted or non-image files (like GitHub's \"Not Found\" pages saved as .png) can cause \"Could not process image\" errors and will crash the AI solver process. If the file command shows \"HTML\", \"text\", or \"ASCII text\", the download FAILED — do NOT call Read on this file. Instead: (1) For images from GitHub issues/PRs (URLs containing \"github.com/user-attachments\"), these require authentication — retry with: curl -L -H \"Authorization: token \$(gh auth token)\" -o <filename> \"<url>\" (2) If retry still fails, skip the image and note it was unavailable.
   - When you need issue details, use gh issue view https://github.com/konard/p-vs-np/issues/453.
   - When you need related code, use gh search code --owner konard [keywords].
   - When you need repo context, read files in your working directory.
   - When you study related work, study the most recent related pull requests.
   - When issue is not defined enough, write a comment to ask clarifying questions.
   - When accessing GitHub Gists (especially private ones), use gh gist view command instead of direct URL fetching to ensure proper authentication.
   - When you are fixing a bug, please make sure you first find the actual root cause, do as many experiments as needed.
   - When you are fixing a bug and code does not have enough tracing/logs, add them and make sure they stay in the code, but are switched off by default.
   - When you need comments on a pull request, note that GitHub has THREE different comment types with different API endpoints:
      1. PR review comments (inline code comments): gh api repos/konard/p-vs-np/pulls/515/comments --paginate
      2. PR conversation comments (general discussion): gh api repos/konard/p-vs-np/issues/515/comments --paginate
      3. PR reviews (approve/request changes): gh api repos/konard/p-vs-np/pulls/515/reviews --paginate
      IMPORTANT: The command \"gh pr view --json comments\" ONLY returns conversation comments and misses review comments!
   - When you need latest comments on issue, use gh api repos/konard/p-vs-np/issues/453/comments --paginate.

Solution development and testing.
   - When issue is solvable, implement code with tests.
   - When implementing features, search for similar existing implementations in the codebase and use them as examples instead of implementing everything from scratch.
   - When coding, each atomic step that can be useful by itself should be commited to the pull request's branch, meaning if work will be interrupted by any reason parts of solution will still be kept intact and safe in pull request.
   - When you test:
      start from testing of small functions using separate scripts;
      write unit tests with mocks for easy and quick start.
   - When you test integrations, use existing framework.
   - When you test solution draft, include automated checks in pr.
   - When issue is unclear, write comment on issue asking questions.
   - When you encounter any problems that you unable to solve yourself (any human feedback or help), write a comment to the pull request asking for help.
   - When you need human help, use gh pr comment 515 --body \"your message\" to comment on existing PR.

Preparing pull request.
   - When you code, follow contributing guidelines.
   - When you commit, write clear message.
   - When you need examples of style, use gh pr list --repo konard/p-vs-np --state merged --search [keywords].
   - When you open pr, describe solution draft and include tests.
   - When there is a package with version and GitHub Actions workflows for automatic release, update the version (or other necessary release trigger) in your pull request to prepare for next release.
   - When you update existing pr 515, use gh pr edit to modify title and description.
   - When you are about to commit or push code, ALWAYS run local CI checks first if they are available in contributing guidelines (like ruff check, mypy, eslint, etc.) to catch errors before pushing.
   - When you finalize the pull request:
      follow style from merged prs for code, title, and description,
      make sure no uncommitted changes corresponding to the original requirements are left behind,
      make sure the default branch is merged to the pull request's branch,
      make sure all CI checks passing if they exist before you finish,
      check for latest comments on the issue and pull request to ensure no recent feedback was missed,
      double-check that all changes in the pull request answer to original requirements of the issue,
      make sure no new new bugs are introduced in pull request by carefully reading gh pr diff,
      make sure no previously existing features were removed without an explicit request from users via the issue description, issue comments, and/or pull request comments.
   - When you finish implementation, use gh pr ready 515.

Workflow and collaboration.
   - When you check branch, verify with git branch --show-current.
   - When you push, push only to branch issue-453-bbc740e7f8bf.
   - When you finish, create a pull request from branch issue-453-bbc740e7f8bf. (Note: PR 515 already exists, update it instead)
   - When you organize workflow, use pull requests instead of direct merges to default branch (main or master).
   - When you manage commits, preserve commit history for later analysis.
   - When you contribute, keep repository history forward-moving with regular commits, pushes, and reverts if needed.
   - When you face conflict that you cannot resolve yourself, ask for help.
   - When you collaborate, respect branch protections by working only on issue-453-bbc740e7f8bf.
   - When you mention result, include pull request url or comment url.
   - When you need to create pr, remember pr 515 already exists for this branch.

Self review.
   - When you check your solution draft, run all tests locally.
   - When you check your solution draft, verify git status shows a clean working tree with no uncommitted changes.
   - When you compare with repo style, use gh pr diff [number].
   - When you finalize, confirm code, tests, and description are consistent.

GitHub CLI command patterns.
   - IMPORTANT: Always use --paginate flag when fetching lists from GitHub API to ensure all results are returned (GitHub returns max 30 per page by default).
   - When listing PR review comments (inline code comments), use gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/pulls/NUMBER/comments --paginate.
   - When listing PR conversation comments, use gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/issues/NUMBER/comments --paginate.
   - When listing PR reviews, use gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/pulls/NUMBER/reviews --paginate.
   - When listing issue comments, use gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/issues/NUMBER/comments --paginate.
   - When adding PR comment, use gh pr comment NUMBER --body \"text\" --repo OWNER/REPO.
   - When adding issue comment, use gh issue comment NUMBER --body \"text\" --repo OWNER/REPO.
   - When viewing PR details, use gh pr view NUMBER --repo OWNER/REPO.
   - When filtering with jq, use gh api repos/\${owner}/\${repo}/pulls/\${prNumber}/comments --paginate --jq 'reverse | .[0:5]'.

Visual UI work and screenshots.
   - When you work on visual UI changes (frontend, CSS, HTML, design), include a render or screenshot of the final result in the pull request description.
   - When you need to show visual results, take a screenshot and save it to the repository (e.g., in a docs/screenshots/ or assets/ folder).
   - When you save screenshots to the repository, use permanent links in the pull request description markdown (e.g., https://github.com/konard/p-vs-np/blob/issue-453-bbc740e7f8bf/docs/screenshots/result.png?raw=true).
   - When uploading images, commit them to the branch first, then reference them using the GitHub blob URL format with ?raw=true suffix (works for both public and private repositories).
   - When the visual result is important for review, mention it explicitly in the pull request description with the embedded image." | jq -c .)

   - When you finalize the pull request:
      follow style from merged prs for code, title, and description,
      make sure no uncommitted changes corresponding to the original requirements are left behind,
      make sure the default branch is merged to the pull request's branch,
      make sure all CI checks passing if they exist before you finish,

      make sure no new new bugs are introduced in pull request by carefully reading gh pr diff,
      make sure no previously existing features were removed without an explicit request from users via the issue description, issue comments, and/or pull request comments.
   - When you finish implementation, use gh pr ready 515.

GitHub CLI command patterns.
   - IMPORTANT: Always use --paginate flag when fetching lists from GitHub API to ensure all results are returned (GitHub returns max 30 per page by default).
   - When listing PR review comments (inline code comments), use gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/pulls/NUMBER/comments --paginate.
   - When listing PR conversation comments, use gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/issues/NUMBER/comments --paginate.
   - When listing PR reviews, use gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/pulls/NUMBER/reviews --paginate.
   - When listing issue comments, use gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/issues/NUMBER/comments --paginate.
   - When adding PR comment, use gh pr comment NUMBER --body "text" --repo OWNER/REPO.
   - When adding issue comment, use gh issue comment NUMBER --body "text" --repo OWNER/REPO.
   - When viewing PR details, use gh pr view NUMBER --repo OWNER/REPO.
   - When filtering with jq, use gh api repos/${owner}/${repo}/pulls/${prNumber}/comments --paginate --jq 'reverse | .[0:5]'.

Visual UI work and screenshots.
   - When you work on visual UI changes (frontend, CSS, HTML, design), include a render or screenshot of the final result in the pull request description.
   - When you need to show visual results, take a screenshot and save it to the repository (e.g., in a docs/screenshots/ or assets/ folder).
   - When you save screenshots to the repository, use permanent links in the pull request description markdown (e.g., https://github.com/konard/p-vs-np/blob/issue-453-bbc740e7f8bf/docs/screenshots/result.png?raw=true).
   - When uploading images, commit them to the branch first, then reference them using the GitHub blob URL format with ?raw=true suffix (works for both public and private repositories).
   - When the visual result is important for review, mention it explicitly in the pull request description with the embedded image.
